---
title: "R Notebook"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

# Dashboards

Dashboards are a useful way to communicate large amounts of information visually and quickly. Create one with the `flexdashboard::flex_dashboard` output format, as in the .Rmd file below (available [here](https://posit.cloud/content/181966) on RStudio Cloud):

![](https://d33wubrfki0l68.cloudfront.net/fb9d9abda3be41ff0159fa0786cecfafcc2ed073/ca334/lesson-images/dashboards-1-dashboard.png)

Flexdashboard makes it easy to organize your content into a visual layout:

-   Each Level 1 Header (`#`) begins a new page in the dashboard.

-   Each Level 2 Header (`##`) begins a new column.

-   Each Level 3 Header (`###`) begins a new box.

You can further modify elements with attributes, as in the `{.sidebar}` above.

Flexdashboard also provides simple tools for creating tabsets, value boxes, and gauges. Learn more about flexdashboard at [flexdashboard: Easy interactive dashboards for R](https://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/flexdashboard/).
